value: ARCH: Client DB (libvex-js Storage.ts) does NOT use migrations. Uses createTable().ifNotExists() on startup. Client DB is a rebuildable cache — sessions re-establish, mail re-downloads, keys re-generate from server state. Only spire (server) needs a formal Kysely migration system.

ARCH DECISION: Decryption belongs in the SDK (libvex), not the apps. VexClient.on('mail') emits DecryptedMail (plaintext). IMail wire format is internal to libvex. Pattern: Signal/libsignal, Matrix/matrix-js-sdk. Key storage at rest is app-specific (KeyStore interface injected into VexClient). New bead vex-chat-7dq implements this: box.ts in packages/crypto, SessionManager in packages/libvex, DecryptedMail type in packages/types. Blocks vex-chat-oz0, 9q0, 14y.

ARCH: SQLite everywhere via Kysely. No IndexedDB, no AsyncStorage for message/session persistence. Platform dialects: better-sqlite3 (Node), expo-sqlite (RN), tauri-plugin-sql (Tauri/desktop). The PersistenceCallbacks layer in @vex-chat/store is a stopgap — once all platforms use IStorage with SQLite, persistence callbacks become unnecessary.

DESKTOP STATUS (2026-04-06): Tauri app fully initializes — SQLite via tauri-plugin-sql, pino logging via tauri-plugin-log, keyring via tauri-plugin-keyring, browser WebSocket. Registration hits spire but gets 400 on POST /register — likely msgpack body encoding issue through Vite proxy. GET /token/register succeeds (200). The ws://localhost:1234 placeholder was fixed. All platform wiring works.

MOBILE STATUS (2026-04-07): expo-sqlite works with STRICT mode (varchar→text, boolean→integer, date→text in DDL). Prekeys, sessions, client init all work. FinalizeScreen has a UI bug (slice of undefined) but the SDK platform stack is fully functional. Double http:// prefix fixed.

PRIVACY: No backups to third-party cloud (S3/B2/GCS). SQLite DB contains plaintext metadata (who-messaged-whom, device info, timestamps). Litestream to S3 violates privacy stance per ADR-002. Backups must stay on local encrypted storage or self-hosted MinIO. GPG-encrypt before any offsite transfer.

WIRE FORMAT: Old spire mail uses packed binary extra field [signKey(32)|ephKey(32)|reserved(32)|AD(68)|otkIdx(6)]=170 bytes. Frame header is HMAC not ephemeral key. mail.sender is deviceID not pubkey. All crypto fields are Uint8Array not hex. New libvex uses hex strings everywhere with ephKey in mail.header and pubkey in mail.sender. spire-wire.ts adapts between formats. See docs/explanation/spire-wire-format.md
